title: Brazil's family health strategy--delivering community-based primary care in a universal health system.

openAccessPdf.disclaimer: Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://api.unpaywall.org/v2/10.1056/NEJMp1501140?email=<INSERT_YOUR_EMAIL> or https://doi.org/10.1056/NEJMp1501140, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. tldr.text: Brazil's national health system has developed and rapidly scaled up an approach to primary care that relies on lay community health agents and interdisciplinary care teams to provide universal access to proactive first-contact care and public health interventions.

journal.name: The New England journal of medicine

abstract: Brazil's national health system has developed and rapidly scaled up an approach to primary care that relies on lay community health agents and interdisciplinary care teams to provide universal access to proactive first-contact care and public health interventions.
